title: Clinical Data Versioning with datom
text: Clinical trials generate evolving data. subjects enroll visits accumulate, EDC (Electronic Data Capture) systems produce periodic data extracts—snapshots grow time. typical Phase II study might 50–100 tables updated monthly, analysis code must work reproducibly snapshots. datom solves version-controlling raw data alongside code. Metadata lives git; actual data lives S3 content-addressed parquet files. Every extract gets SHA-based version, (colleague, regulator) can reproduce historical result. vignette walks realistic workflow: receiving monthly EDC extracts Phase II study, syncing datom, listing tables versions, retrieving historical snapshots version. Note: datom manages raw versioned data. Derived datasets (like ADSL) built downstream daapr ecosystem, reads versioned inputs datom.

title: Clinical Data Versioning with datom
text: datom ships simulated data small Phase II study: 48 subjects enrolled 6 months (Jan–Jun 2026) DM domain: demographics (age, sex, race, country, enrollment date) EX domain: exposure (treatment arm, dose, first dose date) Monthly snapshots: filtering enrollment date simulates receiving updated EDC extracts
code: library(datom)  # View the monthly cutoff dates datom_example_cutoffs() #> month_1    month_2    month_3    month_4    month_5    month_6 #> "2026-01-28" "2026-02-28" "2026-03-28" "2026-04-28" "2026-05-28" "2026-06-28"  # Month-1 extract: only early enrollees dm_m1 <- datom_example_data("dm", cutoff_date = "2026-01-28") nrow(dm_m1) #> [1] 6  # Month-3 extract: enrollment ramps up dm_m3 <- datom_example_data("dm", cutoff_date = "2026-03-28") nrow(dm_m3) #> [1] 19  # Full study data dm_all <- datom_example_data("dm") nrow(dm_all) #> [1] 48

title: Clinical Data Versioning with datom
text: store bundles S3 credentials GitHub PAT single object. Credentials validated construction time — bad credentials fail immediately, mid-workflow.
code: # Build S3 component (same bucket for governance and data in this example) s3 <- datom_store_s3(   bucket     = "clinical-data-bucket",   prefix     = "study-001/",   region     = "us-east-1",   access_key = keyring::key_get("AWS_ACCESS_KEY"),   secret_key = keyring::key_get("AWS_SECRET_KEY") )  # Combine into a store with GitHub PAT for developer role store <- datom_store(   governance = s3,   data       = s3,   github_pat = keyring::key_get("GITHUB_PAT") )

title: Clinical Data Versioning with datom
text: data management team delivers first EDC extract. place files input_files/: Scan sync: just happened: CSV imported via rio::import() written parquet S3 SHA-256 computed source file parquet data metadata.json version_history.json created git synced S3 git commit made pushed table local manifest.json updated current version info
code: # Simulate receiving the month-1 extract cutoffs <- datom_example_cutoffs()  dm_m1 <- datom_example_data("dm", cutoff_date = cutoffs["month_1"]) ex_m1 <- datom_example_data("ex", cutoff_date = cutoffs["month_1"])  write.csv(dm_m1, "study_001_data/input_files/dm.csv", row.names = FALSE) write.csv(ex_m1, "study_001_data/input_files/ex.csv", row.names = FALSE) # Scan input_files/ — computes SHA of each file, compares against manifest manifest <- datom_sync_manifest(conn) #> ℹ Scanned 2 files: 2 new, 0 changed, 0 unchanged. manifest #>   name          file format  file_sha                           status #> 1   dm .../dm.csv    csv    a1b2c3...                          new #> 2   ex .../ex.csv    csv    d4e5f6...                          new  # Sync: imports each file, converts to parquet, uploads to S3, commits to git results <- datom_sync(conn, manifest) #> ℹ Syncing 2 tables... #> ✔ dm synced (new). #> ✔ ex synced (new). #> ℹ Sync complete: 2 succeeded, 0 failed, 0 skipped.

title: Clinical Data Versioning with datom
text: datom_list() gives quick inventory registered table last updated. Version hashes shown 8-character prefixes default — pass short_hash = FALSE full 64-character SHA: Pass include_versions = TRUE see current data SHA alongside version identifier:
code: datom_list(conn) #>   name current_version last_updated           #> 1 dm   a8ee7a31        2026-01-28T10:25:00Z #> 2 ex   1f476930        2026-01-28T10:25:00Z datom_list(conn, include_versions = TRUE) #>   name current_version current_data_sha last_updated          version_count #> 1 dm   a8ee7a31        a1b2c3d4         2026-01-28T10:25:00Z  1 #> 2 ex   1f476930        d4e5f6a7         2026-01-28T10:25:00Z  1

title: Clinical Data Versioning with datom
text: trusting sync worked correctly, read data back spot-check : Rows, columns, date ranges look right. can also read version hash directly — short prefix matching works just like git log --abbrev-commit: Note: version hashes vignette (e.g., "a8ee7a31") illustrative. actual hashes differ derived data content. Always get version hashes datom_list() datom_history() — never copy documentation.
code: # Read the current (month-1) snapshot dm_m1 <- datom_read(conn, "dm") nrow(dm_m1) #> [1] 6  # Spot-check: confirm only early enrollees are present range(as.Date(dm_m1$RFSTDTC)) #> [1] "2026-01-03" "2026-01-28" # Equivalent: read by version hash 8 character prefix # Replace "a8ee7a31" with the hash from your datom_history(conn, "dm") dm_m1 <- datom_read(conn, "dm", version = "a8ee7a31") nrow(dm_m1) #> [1] 6

title: Clinical Data Versioning with datom
text: Two months later, enrollment progressed. data management team sends updated extracts: parquet content changed (new subjects), datom uploaded new parquet files S3 fresh SHAs. old parquet files remain — never overwritten deleted.
code: # Simulate month-3 extract dm_m3 <- datom_example_data("dm", cutoff_date = cutoffs["month_3"]) ex_m3 <- datom_example_data("ex", cutoff_date = cutoffs["month_3"])  write.csv(dm_m3, "study_001_data/input_files/dm.csv", row.names = FALSE) write.csv(ex_m3, "study_001_data/input_files/ex.csv", row.names = FALSE)  # Scan — datom detects both files have changed (different SHA) manifest <- datom_sync_manifest(conn) #> ℹ Scanned 2 files: 0 new, 2 changed, 0 unchanged.  # Sync the updates results <- datom_sync(conn, manifest) #> ℹ Syncing 2 tables... #> ✔ dm synced (changed). #> ✔ ex synced (changed). #> ℹ Sync complete: 2 succeeded, 0 failed, 0 skipped.

title: Clinical Data Versioning with datom
text: datom uses SHA-based content addressing efficient change detection. data hasn’t changed, upload occurs:
code: # Re-sync with the same files — nothing happens manifest <- datom_sync_manifest(conn) #> ℹ Scanned 2 files: 0 new, 0 changed, 2 unchanged.  results <- datom_sync(conn, manifest) #> ℹ All files unchanged. Nothing to sync.

title: Clinical Data Versioning with datom
text: Downstream analysts don’t need git access. connect directly S3 using reader store (github_pat): Important: bucket prefix must exactly match values used datom_init_repo(). Ask data developer ’re unsure. mismatched prefix common source AccessDenied (HTTP 403) errors.
code: # Reader store — no github_pat reader_s3 <- datom_store_s3(   bucket     = "clinical-data-bucket",   prefix     = "study-001/",   access_key = keyring::key_get("AWS_ACCESS_KEY_READER"),   secret_key = keyring::key_get("AWS_SECRET_KEY_READER") )  reader_store <- datom_store(governance = reader_s3, data = reader_s3)  reader_conn <- datom_get_conn(store = reader_store, project_name = "STUDY_001") print(reader_conn) #> ── datom connection #> • Project: "STUDY_001" #> • Role: "reader" #> • Bucket: "clinical-data-bucket" #> • Prefix: "study-001/" #> • Region: "us-east-1"  # List available tables — same as developer tables <- datom_list(reader_conn) #>   name current_version last_updated           #> 1 dm   a8ee7a31        2026-03-28T10:25:00Z #> 2 ex   1f476930        2026-03-28T10:25:00Z  # Read the latest version dm_latest <- datom_read(reader_conn, "dm") nrow(dm_latest) #> [1] 19  # Inspect the full history to find an earlier version datom_history(reader_conn, "dm") #>   version   data_sha  timestamp             author       commit_message #> 1 a8ee7a31  e5f6a7b8  2026-03-28T10:25:00Z  jane@co.com  Sync dm #> 2 f7a8b9c2  a1b2c3d4  2026-01-28T10:25:00Z  jane@co.com  Sync dm  # Read a specific earlier version by hash prefix dm_v1 <- datom_read(reader_conn, "dm", version = "f7a8b9c2") nrow(dm_v1) #> [1] 6

title: Clinical Data Versioning with datom
text: Every data file stored {sha256}.parquet. Two identical datasets always produce SHA, ’s built-deduplication. Different data produces different SHA, versions never collide.

title: Clinical Data Versioning with datom

title: Clinical Data Versioning with datom
text: datom version identifier SHA metadata fields (sorted alphabetically). Since metadata includes data SHA, version uniquely identifies (data, metadata) pair. can use short prefix version hash (like "f7a8b9") — datom resolves long ’s unambiguous, just like git short SHAs.

title: Clinical Data Versioning with datom
text: workflow demonstrates core datom loop clinical data: Build store datom_store() → bundle credentials Initialize datom_init_repo(store = ...) → one-time setup Receive EDC extracts → place input_files/ Scan datom_sync_manifest() → detect new changed files Sync datom_sync() → import, convert parquet, version, upload Explore datom_list(), datom_history(), datom_read() → verify synced moving Update dropping new extracts repeating sync cycle Retrieve historical snapshot datom_read(conn, name, version = "...") Every step tracked git full audit trail. Data readers access version via S3 without needing git credentials. Derived datasets (like ADSL) belong downstream daapr ecosystem, reads versioned raw inputs managed datom.

title: Credential Management
text: datom uses store object manage credentials storage configuration. Credentials passed directly constructing store — magic environment variable naming conventions remember. vignette explains stores work, manage credentials, set access developers readers.

title: Credential Management
text: datom_store bundles everything datom needs connect storage: store two S3 components (governance data) can point different buckets — one. use credentials bucket, can simplify:
code: library(datom)  store <- datom_store(   governance = datom_store_s3(     bucket     = "clinical-data-bucket",     prefix     = "study-001/",     region     = "us-east-1",     access_key = "your_aws_access_key",     secret_key = "your_aws_secret_key"   ),   data = datom_store_s3(     bucket     = "clinical-data-bucket",     prefix     = "study-001/",     region     = "us-east-1",     access_key = "your_aws_access_key",     secret_key = "your_aws_secret_key"   ),   github_pat = "ghp_your_github_pat" ) s3 <- datom_store_s3(   bucket     = "clinical-data-bucket",   prefix     = "study-001/",   access_key = "your_aws_access_key",   secret_key = "your_aws_secret_key" )  store <- datom_store(governance = s3, data = s3, github_pat = "ghp_...")

title: Credential Management
text: datom derives role store: github_pat provided → developer (role = "developer") github_pat omitted → reader (role = "reader")
code: # Developer store dev_store <- datom_store(governance = s3, data = s3, github_pat = "ghp_...") dev_store$role #> [1] "developer"  # Reader store reader_store <- datom_store(governance = s3, data = s3) reader_store$role #> [1] "reader"

title: Credential Management
text: keyring package stores credentials OS keychain (macOS Keychain, Windows Credential Manager, Linux Secret Service): keeps credentials encrypted rest plain-text files.
code: # One-time setup: store credentials keyring::key_set_with_value("AWS_ACCESS_KEY", password = "AKIA...") keyring::key_set_with_value("AWS_SECRET_KEY", password = "wJal...") keyring::key_set_with_value("GITHUB_PAT", password = "ghp_...")  # Build store from keyring (every session) store <- datom_store(   governance = datom_store_s3(     bucket     = "clinical-data-bucket",     prefix     = "study-001/",     access_key = keyring::key_get("AWS_ACCESS_KEY"),     secret_key = keyring::key_get("AWS_SECRET_KEY")   ),   data = datom_store_s3(     bucket     = "clinical-data-bucket",     prefix     = "study-001/",     access_key = keyring::key_get("AWS_ACCESS_KEY"),     secret_key = keyring::key_get("AWS_SECRET_KEY")   ),   github_pat = keyring::key_get("GITHUB_PAT") )

title: Team Collaboration with datom
text: datom supports multiple developers working data repository concurrently. Git tracks metadata; S3 stores data. separation means developers can work parallel long follow simple discipline: pull work, push ’re done. Prerequisites: vignette assumes credentials already configured. See vignette("credentials") setup instructions including AWS keys, github_pat, store construction. vignette covers: One-time repository setup (runs datom_init_repo()) teammates join existing repository daily pull → receive → sync workflow sync rejected (push conflicts) Setting read-access analysts

title: Team Collaboration with datom
text: datom recognizes two user types: Developers local clone git repo. Readers connect directly S3 using datom_get_conn() store project name.

title: Team Collaboration with datom
text: One person initializes repository. Everyone else clones . creates folder structure, creates GitHub repo, pushes initial commit, uploads dispatch.json manifest.json S3.
code: library(datom)  # Build a store (see vignette("credentials") for details) s3 <- datom_store_s3(   bucket     = "clinical-data-bucket",   prefix     = "study-001/",   access_key = keyring::key_get("AWS_ACCESS_KEY"),   secret_key = keyring::key_get("AWS_SECRET_KEY") )  store <- datom_store(   governance = s3,   data       = s3,   github_pat = keyring::key_get("GITHUB_PAT") )  # The lead data engineer runs this once datom_init_repo(   path         = "study_001_data",   project_name = "STUDY_001",   store        = store,   create_repo  = TRUE,   repo_name    = "study-001-data" ) #> ✔ Created GitHub repo "study-001-data" #> ✔ Initialized datom repository at study_001_data #> ✔ Pushed to origin/main #> ✔ Synced dispatch.json and manifest.json to S3

title: Team Collaboration with datom
text: datom_init_repo() checks whether S3 namespace already occupied writing files. another project already claimed s3://clinical-data-bucket/study-001/datom/.metadata/manifest.json, ’ll get clear error: prevents accidental overwrites multiple teams share S3 bucket.
code: datom_init_repo(   path         = "my_project",   project_name = "MY_PROJECT",   store        = store ) #> ✖ S3 namespace already occupied by project "STUDY_001". #>   Location: s3://clinical-data-bucket/study-001/datom/ #> ℹ Use a different bucket or prefix, or pass `.force = TRUE` to override.

title: Team Collaboration with datom
text: Every team member clones datom_clone(). need store remote_url existing repo: datom_clone() wraps git2r::clone() returns ready--use connection — re-initialization needed.

title: Team Collaboration with datom
text: Every work session starts datom_pull(): datom_pull() fetches merges upstream git commits — including metadata changes teammates (manifest updates, new table versions, dispatch changes). Git source truth metadata. ’re already date: Note: don’t strictly need call datom_pull() manually every datom_sync(). datom’s push mechanism automatically pulls pushing ensure ’re date. However, calling datom_pull() start session still recommended — lets see teammates changed begin work.
code: conn <- datom_get_conn(path = "study_001_data")  datom_pull(conn) #> ✔ Pulled 3 commits on "main". #> ℹ Already up to date on "main".

title: Team Collaboration with datom
text: Place new data extracts input_files/, scan sync. intentionally separate steps: scan step produces manifest can inspect committing sync. checkpoint: verify right files detected statuses (new, changed, unchanged) make sense writing anything git S3. two steps? Separating scan sync gives chance review synced anything written. team setting especially valuable — can catch stale files, accidental duplicates, unexpected changes hit git S3. Future versions datom expand manifest-level APIs richer pre-sync inspection (e.g., adding table descriptions, tagging QA status).
code: # Example: write a data extract to the input directory dm <- datom_example_data("dm", cutoff_date = "2026-03-28") write.csv(dm, "study_001_data/input_files/dm.csv", row.names = FALSE)  # Scan — detects new and changed files in input_files/ manifest <- datom_sync_manifest(conn)  # Inspect before syncing — what did datom find? manifest #>   table_name status      file_path #> 1         dm changed  input_files/dm.csv # Sync — imports, converts to parquet, writes to git + S3 results <- datom_sync(conn, manifest) #> ℹ Syncing 1 table... #> ✔ dm synced (changed). #> ℹ Sync complete: 1 succeeded, 0 failed, 0 skipped.

text: datom_validate() confirms local git state S3 agree — metadata matches data, referenced parquet files exist S3, nothing missing orphaned. Run every sync session. ’s . sync committed git, pushed, uploaded data + metadata S3. teammates can now datom_pull() see new version.
code: # Check repository consistency datom_validate(conn) #> ✔ All checks passed. Git and S3 are consistent.

title: Team Collaboration with datom
text: teammate pushed syncing, datom_sync() abort clear message: Resolution:
code: #> ✖ Local git branch "main" is behind remote by 2 commits. #> ℹ Run `datom_pull()` or `git pull` to update before syncing. # 1. Pull the latest changes datom_pull(conn)  # 2. Re-scan (manifest may have changed) manifest <- datom_sync_manifest(conn)  # 3. Re-sync results <- datom_sync(conn, manifest)

title: Team Collaboration with datom
text: Merge conflicts occur two developers modify file (e.g., update metadata.json table). rare practice datom auto-generates metadata files — can happen developers sync table simultaneously. Resolution: Open terminal repo directory Run git status see conflicting files Edit files resolve conflicts (accept one side git checkout --) git add resolved files git commit Re-run datom_sync() Tip: Merge conflicts minimized developer responsible different tables. Coordinate syncs tables within team.
code: #> ✖ Merge conflict detected — manual resolution required. #> ℹ Pull latest changes, resolve conflicts, and re-run. #> ℹ Use `git status` to see conflicting files.

title: Team Collaboration with datom

code: # Reader store — no github_pat reader_s3 <- datom_store_s3(   bucket     = "clinical-data-bucket",   prefix     = "study-001/",   access_key = keyring::key_get("AWS_ACCESS_KEY_READER"),   secret_key = keyring::key_get("AWS_SECRET_KEY_READER") )  reader_store <- datom_store(governance = reader_s3, data = reader_s3)  reader_conn <- datom_get_conn(store = reader_store, project_name = "STUDY_001") print(reader_conn) #> ── datom connection #> • Project: "STUDY_001" #> • Role: "reader" #> • Bucket: "clinical-data-bucket" #> • Prefix: "study-001/" #> • Region: "us-east-1" datom_list(reader_conn) datom_read(reader_conn, "dm") datom_history(reader_conn, "dm")

title: Copilot Instructions for datom
text: cli pluralization: {?s} requires quantity reference immediately (e.g., {length(x)} variable{?s}). Without quantity, cli throws confusing error. git2r::default_signature(): Fails freshly git2r::init()’d repos lack local config. Always call git2r::config(repo, user.name = ..., user.email = ...) init. git2r::merge(): Expects string (branch name), branch object. Use upstream_ref$name. cli dot-literals: cli >= 3.4.0, {.something} inside cli_*() call interpreted cli style, expression. Wrap function call variable starting . parentheses: {(.datom_build_storage_key(...))}, {(.sandbox_storage_label(store$data))}. applies cli_li, cli_alert_*, cli_abort, etc. — just cli_abort. .datom_git_commit() idempotent: Returns HEAD SHA (instead erroring) staged files unchanged. design — enables safe re-runs partial failures local → git → S3 pipeline. metadata SHA uses JSON canonical form: .datom_compute_metadata_sha() hashes jsonlite::toJSON() output serialize = FALSE, R object. critical — R’s serialize() type-sensitive (10L ≠ 10), metadata round-tripped JSON produce different SHA. Always test SHA stability JSON round-trip. metadata SHA excludes volatile fields: created_at datom_version stripped hashing. Adding new metadata fields affect versioning must added volatile vector .datom_compute_metadata_sha(). version_history dedup guard: .datom_write_metadata_local() skips appending latest entry version SHA. prevents duplicates means guard relies metadata_sha correctness. datom_pull() git-: S3 manifest refresh — git source truth metadata. manifest committed git pulled everything else. S3 namespace check swallows connectivity errors: .datom_check_namespace_free() datom_init_repo() warns doesn’t fail network errors — offline init still works, S3 push fail later anyway. git2r::clone() target path: Must exist must empty directory. datom_clone() validates upfront. paws.storage STS: sts paws.security.identity, paws.storage. Validation uses HeadBucket (validates credentials bucket access). Storage abstraction: Business logic must call .datom_storage_*(), never .datom_s3_*() .datom_local_*() directly. dispatch layer R/utils-storage.R routes based conn$backend. datom_conn two clients: client (data store) gov_client (governance store). Use .datom_gov_conn() create sub-connection governance operations. conn$root backend-neutral: S3: root = bucket name. Local: root = directory path. conn$client NULL local backend: .datom_local_*() functions use conn$root + conn$prefix directly via fs::. Never check .null(conn$client) determine backend — use conn$backend instead. datom_store_local$path vs datom_store_s3$bucket: Store components backend-specific field names. Use .datom_store_root() accessor backend-neutral access. ref.json lives governance store: Created datom_init_repo(), resolved .datom_resolve_ref(). Contains current data location (bucket/prefix/region). Ref resolution asymmetry: Conn-time ref failure warn-(governance informs, gate). Write-time ref failure hard abort reason (.datom_check_ref_current()) — writing without verified location risks orphaning data, safe fallback. Reads don’t re-check — stale conn fails cleanly user rebuilds. Migration detection role-aware: .datom_resolve_data_location() compares store$data location vs ref location. Developer mismatch → auto-pull git re-read project.yaml (errors still disagrees). Reader mismatch → warn + proceed ref-resolved location using reader’s existing credentials. Backend labels lookup-based, binary: Prefer c(s3 = "S3", local = "local")[conn$backend] %||% conn$backend (conn$backend == "s3") ... else .... New backends (GCS, etc.) become one-line additions. Applies UI strings; dispatch still uses switch() utils-storage.R. datom_init_repo() validates side effects: store/repo validation happens filesystem git operations. failure, nothing left behind. project.yaml two-component structure: storage.governance + storage.data — type, bucket, prefix, region. Secrets never persisted. _pkgdown.yml index must kept sync: Adding new exported symbol requires matching entry _pkgdown.yml. pkgdown::build_site() errors “N topics missing index” otherwise. Check every phase adds exports. Non-ASCII characters R source: R CMD check warns non-ASCII character R/*.R files (even comments). Use ASCII — -- instead —, -> instead →.

code: library(datom)  # Build a store with credentials s3 <- datom_store_s3(   bucket     = "my-bucket",   prefix     = "data/",   access_key = keyring::key_get("AWS_ACCESS_KEY"),   secret_key = keyring::key_get("AWS_SECRET_KEY") )  store <- datom_store(   governance = s3,   data       = s3,   github_pat = keyring::key_get("GITHUB_PAT") )  # Initialize a datom repository datom_init_repo(   path         = "my_project",   project_name = "MYPROJ",   store        = store,   create_repo  = TRUE,   repo_name    = "my-project-data" )  # Get connection conn <- datom_get_conn(path = "my_project", store = store)  # Sync input files to versioned storage manifest <- datom_sync_manifest(conn) datom_sync(conn, manifest)  # Write individual tables datom_write(conn, data = my_data, name = "customers", message = "Initial load")

title: Version-Controlled Data Management for Reproducible Workflows
code: library(datom)  # Build a read-only store (no github_pat) reader_s3 <- datom_store_s3(   bucket     = "my-bucket",   prefix     = "data/",   access_key = keyring::key_get("AWS_ACCESS_KEY"),   secret_key = keyring::key_get("AWS_SECRET_KEY") )  reader_store <- datom_store(governance = reader_s3, data = reader_s3)  # Connect directly to S3 conn <- datom_get_conn(   store        = reader_store,   project_name = "MYPROJ" )  # List available tables datom_list(conn)  # Read current version customers <- datom_read(conn, "customers")  # Read specific version for reproducibility customers_v1 <- datom_read(conn, "customers", version = "abc123...")
